Ad Text:
About Us

At Red Energy, we are passionate about providing award-winning customer service to over 1.5 million of our customers across the country. Owned by the mighty Snowy Hydro, we are 100% Australian owned which our customers love us for. Our award winning team have won 15 consecutive Canstar Blue Awards for best overall customer satisfaction amongst Victorian electricity providers. Ready for a new career - come grow with us!

About the Role

We are looking for a strategic and driven Pricing Manager to lead our  Pricing team. Reporting to the Manager Pricing & Customer Analysis, you will be responsible for spearheading pricing strategies that improve market competitiveness while offering superior value to our retail customers.

In this role, you will empower a high-performing team of analysts to transform complex market data into actionable commercial insights and predictive models. You will act as a trusted advisor across the business, identifying new growth opportunities while ensuring our pricing framework remains robust, compliant, and agile. This is a pivotal leadership position where your ability to refine our systems and mentor your team will directly shape our future profitability and the experience of our 1.5 million customers.

Enjoy flexible working arrangements with 3 days in the office, 2 days remote.

In this role, you will:

Manage pricing strategies related to customer profitability, segmentation, and predictive modeling.
Lead, motivate, and coach a team of Analysts to achieve growth targets and deliver an exceptional customer experience.
Provide high-level market analytics to support strategic pricing decisions and contribute to budgeting and forecasting assumptions.
Identify opportunities to streamline pricing processes and cultivate innovative prospects for new business development.
Maintain a clear understanding of regulatory and compliance requirements and ensure all pricing timetables are met.
Proactively collaborate with internal customers to identify opportunities backed by analytical evidence.

What we’re looking for:

Significant experience in business modeling or pricing, ideally within the energy or utilities sector.
A relevant degree in Mathematics, Finance, Data Science, etc is desirable.
A natural ability to influence others, solve complex problems, and adapt to changing business requirements.
A high level of attention to detail and a commitment to maintaining the quality of pricing research and market analysis.

If you are ready to lead a team that sits at the heart of our commercial success and help shape the future of energy in Australia, we’d love to hear from you.

Our client, a leading Top-Tier Financial Services organisation, is looking for a sharp, detail-oriented Business Analyst to join their Property Technology Delivery team. This is a high-impact role sitting at the intersection of physical infrastructure and digital connectivity, primarily supporting a major brand acquisition integration alongside a massive portfolio of national property initiatives and offices.
The Opportunity
You won't just be sitting in one lane. This role manages requirements across approximately 20 concurrent projects, ranging from large-scale office relocations to high-tech upgrades within 70+ national offices. While many BA roles lean heavily into software, this position is focused on the "Guts and Gears" of physical spaces-think Networking, IT Infrastructure, and Property Technology.
Key Responsibilities
Support a diverse project pipeline across Australia and New Zealand.
Join the Discovery Team to conduct initial assessments and perform complex funding calculations. You'll play a gatekeeper role in determining which projects are viable for FY27.
Elicit and document rigorous technical requirements. We don't just need someone to take notes; we need someone who asks "Why?" to ensure every solution is fit for purpose.
Work closely with a high-performing team of 10-15 professionals across Sydney, Melbourne, and NZ.
What You Bring
To be successful in this mix of property and tech, you'll need:
Proven experience in Property, Networking, or IT Infrastructure projects
Experience within Financial Services or large-scale Enterprise organisations is highly preferred
Exceptional attention to detail, specifically regarding funding models and technical requirements
A track record of digging deeper into technical requirements rather than just scratching the surface
The Logistics
$825 per day + Super
Hybrid - Melbourne office 50%
Initial 6-month engagement with potential to extend
Join the Team
This role has become available because the previous BA was so successful they moved into a permanent position within the business. If you are a BA who thrives in a fast-paced, multi-project environment, we want to hear from you.

Call James on 0452 393959 or Apply for more information

Job Title: Project Manager with a Business Analysis Focus
Ad Text:
About Clayton Utz

Clayton Utz is one of Australia's leading law firms, known for its commitment to excellence, innovation and collaboration. We invest in our people and in the technology that supports the delivery of outstanding legal services to our clients. Our Information Technology (IT) team plays a central role in driving that investment forward.

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Project Manager with a strong Business Analyst focus to join our IT team in Sydney on a full-time, permanent basis. In this role, you will play a key part in shaping and delivering technology initiatives across the firm by translating business needs into clear, well-defined requirements.

Your work will support high-quality and efficient delivery, reduce implementation risks and improve how the firm leverages technology through strong analysis, stakeholder engagement and solution design support.

Key Responsibilities

Lead requirements analysis across technology initiatives, including facilitating workshops, mapping current and future state processes, and ensuring requirements are clear, prioritised and aligned to business objectives.
Partner with developers, architects and vendors to translate requirements into fit-for-purpose solution designs and technical artefacts.
Build trusted relationships with IT and business stakeholders, facilitating discussions and providing clear recommendations to support good decisions.
Develop test scenarios and coordinate user acceptance testing to ensure solutions meet agreed requirements.
Support implementation and delivery within agreed scope and budget, including resolving issues as they arise.
Identify opportunities to improve business processes and system use, and support initiatives that drive efficiency, automation and better technology outcomes.


About You

To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Relevant tertiary qualifications in IT, Business Information Systems or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.
Professional certifications in project management (such as PMP, PRINCE2 or AIPM RegPM) are required with experience in Agile delivery.
Business Analysis qualifications (such as IIBA or CBAP), Agile certifications or Design Thinking training are desirable.
Proven experience gathering, analysing and documenting business and system requirements, including process maps and functional specifications.
Experience supporting technology implementations and process improvements, including test scenario development and UAT coordination.
Demonstrated ability to engage and influence stakeholders to efficiently define and validate requirements.
Strong communication and relationship-building skills, with the ability to collaborate and communicate cross-functionally.
Awareness and curiosity towards AI and automation in business, with a forward-thinking mindset and genuine interest in how AI and automation are changing the way professional services firms work.
Strong analytical, problem-solving and organisational skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities and deliver high-quality outcomes.


Why Clayton Utz?

At Clayton Utz, you will join a firm that values innovation, collaboration and professional development. You will have the opportunity to work on meaningful technology initiatives that directly support the delivery of legal services, in a supportive and collegiate team environment.

When you join us, you will enjoy:

Access to professional development programs and career growth opportunities.
The resources and infrastructure to perform your role well, including access to Harvey, our AI platform.
A focus on health and wellbeing, with initiatives to support your physical and mental health including lifestyle and gym membership discounts via our partnership with AIA Vitality.
A collaborative and inclusive culture that celebrates diversity and fosters belonging.
Up to 26 weeks' paid parental leave, with flexibility over two years, plus paid superannuation.
The option to purchase up to 4 weeks of additional leave annually.

At ASIC, you can be the change that ensures a fair, strong and efficient financial system for the benefit of all Australians. Contribute to delivering on ASIC's purpose, vision, and strategic priorities to help maintain the integrity of the financial system and protect consumers from harm.

 

As a Senior Analyst (Strategic Initiatives), you will lead and contribute to high‑impact work responding to structural change across the Australian financial markets system, aligned with ASIC's strategic objectives. In this role within the Markets Group, you will deliver strategic policy, with a focus on the efficiency and resilience of Australia's financial market structure.

 

Location - Brisbane, Canberra, Sydney or Melbourne
Salary between $152,074 - $168,164
Permanent
Applications close at 11:59pm ADST/AEST, 7 May 2026

 

The team 

ASIC's Markets Group is responsible for ensuring confidence and integrity across Australia's financial markets and broader financial system. The Group regulates market participants and infrastructure and delivers supervision, licensing and regulatory reform to support market integrity, transparency and resilience.

The Strategic Initiatives team leads cross‑cutting work across the Markets Group, focusing on emerging risks and structural change affecting markets, coordinating high‑priority initiatives (such as digital assets reforms), and driving alignment and impact across ASIC.

 

The role

As a Senior Analyst, you will be accountable for delivering strategic initiatives and regulatory reform work across Markets Group.

You will be accountable for:

Leading and contributing to strategic policy, supervision and regulatory reform initiatives across Markets Group's remit within the Australian financial markets system.
With a focus on market structure in financial markets, undertake research and policy analysis to assess the efficiency, competitive landscape and resilience of Australia's markets.
Managing complex projects and workstreams, identifying key dependencies, risks, issues and opportunities to maximise regulatory impact.
Developing and delivering policy, implementation and law reform advice in response to emerging and evolving regulatory and market issues.
Working collaboratively across ASIC to support coordinated delivery of interconnected initiatives.
Providing judgement and input on high‑priority regulatory matters in consultation with senior leaders and key stakeholders.

 

About you

You bring strong experience delivering regulatory reform, policy implementation, supervision or licensing work within complex environments. You have sound knowledge of the Australian financial markets system and demonstrate genuine curiosity about emerging issues, market developments and new areas of regulatory focus. You seek to understand how issues connect across the system, are comfortable exploring new problems, testing assumptions and developing practical responses, and can manage multiple priorities while applying critical analysis and judgement. Strong project management capability and experience contributing to strategic initiatives are essential.

You work effectively both independently and collaboratively, maintain a clear focus on delivery and progress, engage constructively on complex or contested matters, and contribute clear, well‑reasoned advice. You build credibility through sound judgement, curiosity, collaboration and consistent delivery.

 

View the position description for more information or click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications close at 11:59pm ADST/AEST, 7 May 2026.

 

About ASIC

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms.

 

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Applications from people of all ages, cultural backgrounds, abilities, LGBTIQ+ identity, and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent are encouraged to apply.

We offer a range of employee benefits including:

Attractive superannuation
Additional leave entitlements
50/50 hybrid work-from home model
Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

 

To work with us, you need to be an Australian citizen and be prepared to complete an ASIC Suitability and Baseline Assessment.

Company Description

Our collaborative culture sets us apart. We help experts become true specialists and provide the long-term structure and platform to accelerate their success.

Job Description

Colliers is seeking an experienced technology platforms professional to manage and deliver the applications, systems and reporting data, and analytics solutions that support our Occupier Services Clients across Australia.

The position is centered on delivering reliable, scalable technology enablement for Occupier Clients - combining multi-client dashboard oversight with stewardship of Integrated Workplace Management Solution (IWMS) platforms that underpin Portfolio Strategy, Lease Administration, Accounts Payable and Transaction Management services.

You will work closely with our Enterprise Clients teams, business stakeholders, and Colliers technology functions to manage and enhance our technology offerings, ensuring they are operationally sound and effectively delivered.

Own and manage core IWMS platforms enabling portfolio management.
Lead technology projects through application of project management and requirements elicitation skills, including system upgrades, and new platform rollouts.
Manage day‑to‑day performance of key lease, portfolio, and transaction systems.
Manage BI and analytics outputs, including dashboards, reporting, and hands‑on data work using Excel, SQL, and BI tools.
Support business development by providing platform expertise, demonstrations, and input into RFPs and proposals.
Drive solution design, client onboarding, system adoption, training, and ongoing technology support.
Support client-facing teams by delivering technology demonstrations and contributing to solution design for prospective and existing clients.
Qualifications
5 – 10 years’ experience in technology platforms, applications support, systems delivery roles.
Demonstrated experience administering enterprise platforms (IWMS, ERP, or similar).
Bachelor’s degree in IT, Information Systems, Business, or a related discipline.
Experience in real estate, property, or professional services preferred.
Demonstrated experience supporting client-facing teams and commercial processes.
Confident with presenting and demonstrating platforms to clients and non-technical users.

Technical Capability

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
Strong working knowledge of SQL and relational databases.
Solid understanding of the Microsoft ecosystem (Microsoft 365, Power BI; Azure concepts desirable).

What’s in It for you?

Named Forbes World's Best Employers 2025 & TIME World's Best Companies 2025!
Colliers Balance - managing home & work life.
Wide range of benefits designed to support your career, wellbeing, and lifestyle.
Industry leading leave – including 26 weeks of parental leave, additional paid leave, volunteering, and loyalty leave.
Formal career development & recognition programs.
Innovative technology & tools to drive your productivity.
Additional Information

We foster an environment in which everyone at Colliers feels respected and comfortable bringing their authentic selves to do their best work. It’s our people who set us apart and we actively recruit people who represent the diversity of the communities in which we operate. We take great pride in doing what’s right and operating in ways that benefit the business, our people, our clients, our communities, and the environment.

Experts join experts, and we welcome you to join us as we lead the industry into the future.

Please be advised that applications will only be accepted directly rather than via recruitment agencies.

Who is Plenti? 

Plenti is a fintech lender, providing faster, fairer loans by leveraging its smart technology. Plenti is a dynamic and innovative business that is growing strongly. By continuing to deliver better customer experiences, Plenti is taking market share from incumbent players in the personal lending, renewable energy, and automotive finance markets.

We are a fast moving and ambitious business that seeks to recruit smart and capable people, who can take ownership of their role to help the business thrive. With over 250 people based in Australia, Plenti is of a size where everyone can make a difference in their role and help us realise our very big ambitions as a team, as we go about building Australia’s best lender.

Plenti is a founder led business that launched in 2014, listed on the ASX since 2020 with annual revenue of over $250 million and a loan portfolio of over $2.5 billion.


About the role:
This is a high-impact opportunity to shape how our growing business scales, operates, and delivers value. As Manager, Continuous Improvement, you’ll partner with senior leaders to identify inefficiencies, improve processes, and deliver measurable performance outcomes.

You’ll lead initiatives end-to-end, combining structured problem-solving with hands-on execution, while also helping to build and embed frameworks, tools, and governance that support continuous improvement across the business!

Key responsibilities:
Shaping the Continuous Improvement Function 

Help define and embed Plenti’s organisation-wide approach to continuous improvement, including frameworks, governance, tools, and templates

Maintain a pipeline of improvement opportunities, ensuring initiatives are prioritised based on strategic value and financial impact

Champion a culture of continuous improvement across the business, coaching and supporting teams to identify and act on improvement opportunities in their day-to-day work

Continuous Improvement Delivery

Identify and analyse existing business processes and ways of working across Plenti’s organisation, using data and structured diagnostics to surface inefficiencies and improvement opportunities

Design and implement practical, scalable improvements that reduce operational costs and improve how work gets done

Lead end-to-end delivery of improvement initiatives, from problem definition and solution design through to implementation and benefits realisation

Track and report on the operational and financial impact of improvement initiatives, ensuring benefits are clearly defined, measured, and realised

Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a trusted partner to senior leaders across Plenti, helping them identify where processes are holding the business back and how to fix them

Build strong working relationships across functions including operations, sales, technology, finance and compliance to understand workflows and co-design solutions

Communicate clearly and compellingly on findings, recommendations, and progress to the senior leadership team and executive


About you: 
You bring a sharp, analytical mindset and a genuine drive to make things happen. You thrive in ambiguity, quickly cutting through complexity to create clarity, and turn ideas into action with pace and confidence. 

Essential Experience:

5+ years’ experience in consulting, operational excellence, continuous improvement, or similar roles

Proven track record of delivering measurable improvements such as cost reduction, efficiency gains, or operational uplift

Strong process mapping and problem-solving capability—you can break down complex problems into clear, actionable solutions

Experience applying Lean, Six Sigma, or similar frameworks in real-world environments

Excellent communication skills, with the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders

Interest or experience in AI and automation as drivers of productivity

Experience in financial services, lending, or fintech environments (Desirable)

Exposure to technology-led improvements such as automation, workflow tools, system integration or digital transformation (Desirable)


What's life like at Plenti? 

Working at Plenti means joining a team that is high-performing, diverse, customer-focused and collaborative. It’s a place where everyone can “make it happen” and share in the results and success. If being in a dynamic industry as part of a market-leading fintech sounds like you, then join to help us reshape Australia’s financial eco-system for the good.

In return, you will enjoy a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship to take your career further. We offer flexible working and volunteer and study leave to develop your skills. We also have monthly social events, office snacks, company Townhalls and D&I initiatives and events to help you connect with your colleagues. 

Plenti is committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce. 

We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people with disabilities. When you apply, let us know of any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process so we can accommodate you.

dbt

Shape the direction of the small-to-medium business segment and lead its pricing strategy
Apply deep analytical thinking to build sharper small-business pricing outcomes
Attractive hybrid work model | Energy Discounts | Career Growth Opportunities

Alinta Energy is one of Australia’s biggest energy retailers, generators and developers with over 1.1 million customers. Australia’s energy system is undergoing the biggest transformation in its history and we’re right there with it. We’re investing in and developing large-scale renewables and storage projects as part of our ongoing effort to provide our customers with reliable, affordable and sustainable energy.

We understand that flexibility matters when considering your next role. Let us know your requirements, and we’ll carefully consider how we might accommodate them.

Ready to make a real impact?

Alinta Energy is hiring a dedicated SME Pricing Analyst based in Sydney or Melbourne, to accelerate growth in our small business customer segment. This role sits at the intersection of pricing development, commercial analysis and system enablement, with a strong initial focus on building and embedding robust, bottom-up pricing capability and helping design and establish the system functionality and processes needed to support a deeper and more tailored approach to small business pricing.

Reporting directly to the Head of Pricing, you’ll shape pricing direction for this segment by constructing transparent, assumption-driven pricing models that clearly link cost inputs, margin targets and final price outcomes, guide how pricing is delivered through our systems, and operate with a high level of autonomy in a role that plays a foundational part in how we grow and compete in the small business market.

You’ll turn complex cost, customer and regulatory inputs into clear, commercially sound pricing outcomes with models designed to flex as assumptions evolve, while partnering closely with teams across Sales, Product, Customer Strategy, Wholesale and Billing to drive effective execution.

Why you’ll love working here

At Alinta Energy, we’re taking real action to deliver reliable and sustainable energy for Australia - and that practical approach runs through everything we do. You’ll get clear direction, genuine flexibility, and the support you need to grow, all while working on projects that make a lasting difference. We’re big enough to create impact, but small enough that your ideas and effort will always be seen.

There are plenty of other great benefits of working for Alinta Energy, including flexible working, energy discounts, an Employee Assistance Program (EAP), a paid volunteer day each year, fully stocked kitchens, and lots more.

What you'll do

Own commercial pricing outcomes for the small business segment, translating cost drivers, regulatory constraints, competitive dynamics, and margin objectives into optimised price structures and recommendations with clear rationale.
Build and maintain bottom up pricing models that clearly link wholesale, network, retail, and operational cost inputs to margin targets and final customer prices, with the flexibility to test scenarios as assumptions shift.
Identify revenue, margin, and volume optimisation opportunities by analysing customer behaviour, load profiles, tariff structures, and competitor positioning, and quantify trade offs to support clear commercial decisions.
Shape the pricing capability for a new and growing segment, working closely with technology and operational teams to define requirements, guide system build, and ensure pricing logic, controls, and governance are fit for scale.
Establish repeatable pricing processes and frameworks that support effective execution today and a smooth transition into BAU as the segment grows.
Partner with Sales, Product, Customer Strategy, Wholesale, Networks, and Billing to validate assumptions, align on commercial objectives, and ensure pricing outcomes are executable and market competitive.
Operate with autonomy and commercial judgement, setting priorities, communicating recommendations clearly, and continuously improving pricing performance, processes, and tools.

Your experience

Strong quantitative and financial modelling capability, ideally with a degree in commerce, economics, accounting, maths or similar
Proven analytical ability and commercial acumen - able to translate complex cost, regulatory and competitive factors into clear pricing recommendations, with the ability to explain how those inputs flow through to margin and final price.
Excellent communication skills, capable of simplifying complexity for senior leaders, technical teams and cross-functional stakeholders.
Experience working autonomously in environments that require process creation, system understanding and structured methodical execution.
Strong problem solving capability, with the ability to navigate ambiguity, work through incomplete information, and develop practical, commercially sound solutions
Energy retail or SME-segment experience (pricing, billing, settlements or product) highly desirable but not essential if you bring the right quantitative skills

Please note this role requires full working rights within Australia.

Like what you’re seeing?

If you like the sound of this, and you’ve got experience in a similar role, we’d love to talk to you. Come and discover better together with Alinta Energy.

We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we commit to respond to every applicant.

If your application progresses, we will need to complete background and pre-employment checks, such as immigration checks, reference checks, criminal background checks, qualification checks, and additional checks for roles related to finance.

Our Diversity Commitment

Alinta Energy offers a truly diverse, safe and supportive work environment. As an equal opportunity employer, Alinta Energy encourages applications from any qualified person, including First Nations Australians, members of the LGBTQIA+ community, people of all ages and people living with a disability. We are also proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer and we are committed to advancing women’s careers.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Ad Text:
Employment Type: Full Time Permanent
Position Classification: Health Manager Level 4
Remuneration: $147,653 - $175,917 per annum plus Superannuation
Location: 1 Reserve Road, St Leonards NSW
Hours Per Week: 38 hours
Requisition ID:REQ656682

Join our Business Analytics & Intelligence Team (BAIT) at Ministry of Health for an exciting Full Time Permanent opportunity and be part of the team enriching health in millions of ways every day.  

About us

We are committed to your growth and development, providing the resources and opportunities you need to excel in your career.
Join a passionate team dedicated to innovation and creativity.
While regular office presence is essential, we offer flexible work arrangements to support your needs.


To learn more about the Ministry and what benefits we offer, access our Employee Benefits Handbook.

About the role:
With your skills and experience, you can contribute to the millions of ways we’re enhancing health and wellness while developing 
your career as part of the largest health organisation in Australia.

Be part of a team that turns complex financial data into clear, trusted insights that support critical decision making across NSW Health. As a Senior Statistician in Financial Planning and Analytics, you will apply advanced statistical and analytical methods to inform patient costing, funding models and system performance. Your work will help decision makers understand trends, trade offs and impacts across the health system, supporting evidence based planning and resource allocation. You will collaborate with experienced analysts and stakeholders across districts, pillars and the Ministry to ensure insights are robust, transparent and fit for purpose. The role offers exposure to large scale, real world data challenges and the opportunity to deepen your expertise in health system analytics. If you are driven by analytical rigour, relevance and impact, this role provides a meaningful platform to shape better decisions through data.

Health Manager Level 4, starting salary from $147,653 - $175,917 per annum plus Superannuation, with a 38-hour work week.

What you’ll be doing

Extract, transform and statistically analyse large, complex health datasets to support patient costing, funding analysis and system level decision making. 
Work collaboratively with other BAIT team members to reconcile multi source data, resolve data quality issues and ensure analytical consistency. 
Apply advanced statistical techniques to identify trends, variances and cost drivers across activity based funding and patient care streams. 
Produce reproducible, well documented analytical outputs that support routine reporting, modelling and ad hoc analytical requests. 
Translate complex analytical findings into clear, decision ready insights for financial and funding stakeholders across NSW Health.

 

About you:
We are seeking motivated and committed individuals who can support the work of NSW Health with: 

Tertiary qualifications in statistics, health information management, data science or a related quantitative discipline, or equivalent demonstrated experience.
Strong experience extracting, validating and analysing high volume health datasets using tools such as SAS, SQL, Python/R and Excel.
Sound knowledge of patient costing, activity based funding and health system data, with an understanding of data governance and quality frameworks.
Proven ability to work collaboratively with other team members to reconcile multi source data and produce robust, reproducible analysis.
Demonstrated capability to translate complex statistical outputs into clear, decision ready insights for technical and non technical stakeholders.


How to apply
At The Ministry of Health, we want you to make the most of the opportunities you’ll get to grow your skills and your career, surrounded by authentic, smart people and leaders.
Please review the role description and submit your up-to-date resume (up to 5 pages) and cover letter (1-2 pages) AND demonstrate your ability by attaching a response to the two target questions below.

Target Questions (1-2 pages)

Describe your experience extracting, validating and analysing large, multi source datasets to support patient costing or activity based funding and include what tools and techniques did you use, and how did you ensure data quality?
Provide an example of how you worked collaboratively with analytics colleagues to produce robust, reproducible analysis and translate it into clear insights for financial or funding related decision making.


Use the link below to access our Capability Application tool, designed to help you excel in the recruitment process. Practice your responses with the example questions provided to effectively highlight your achievements and the strategies you used to accomplish them.  

Capability application tool | NSW Public Service Commission

Monitor BWS pricing performance against financial targets and customer strategies.
Improve operational efficiency through process innovation and capability uplift in pricing.

Role model Endeavour Group’s Leadership Values and support the broader Customer Value agenda.

Now let’s talk about you:

Proven strategy implementation experience.
Strong analytical skills, including proficiency in financial modeling and data analysis tools.

About the Company

Our client is a large, customer‑focused organisation operating at national scale and undergoing significant transformation.

About the Role

Our client is establishing a greenfield AI capability and is seeking an AI Transformation Lead to operate at the intersection of business transformation, AI solution delivery, and change management.

This role is not hands‑on engineering. Instead, you will work closely with business units, subject matter experts and engineering teams to identify AI opportunities, redesign workflows for human + AI collaboration, and ensure solutions are successfully adopted and optimised post‑launch.

The focus is on embedding AI into real operational and customer environments at scale, delivering measurable outcomes rather than proofs of concept.

Key Responsibilities:


Identify and shape high‑value AI use cases across operations, internal productivity and customer experiences.
Translate business problems into clear AI solution definitions and delivery plans.
Redesign end‑to‑end workflows to embed human‑AI collaboration.
Partner closely with AI engineering teams to guide solution delivery.
Lead adoption, training and change activities to embed AI into BAU.
Establish feedback loops and drive continuous optimisation post‑launch.
Track benefits and outcomes including efficiency, cost and experience improvements.
Engage confidently with senior stakeholders across business and technology.

About You


Significant prior experience delivering digital, AI or technology‑enabled transformation in complex organisations.
Proven experience taking AI or automation initiatives from concept through to production and adoption.
Strong ability to translate business challenges into structured AI use cases.
Experience redesigning workflows and embedding new ways of working.
Solid conceptual understanding of modern AI (e.g. LLMs, RAG, agentic workflows, prompting).
Strong stakeholder engagement, communication and delivery skills.
Backgrounds from consulting, transformation, product or innovation roles are well suited.
Technical/development background (Advantageous)

The Benefits


Permanent opportunity to shape a greenfield AI function
High‑visibility role with strong executive backing
Hybrid working arrangement
Competitive salary package
Long‑term career growth as the AI capability scales

Solve by Talent is partnering with the University of Sydney to recruit a Manager, Research Systems to support the delivery of key Information and Communications Technology (ICT) initiatives that help shape the future of education, research, and innovation. 
  
Why join the University of Sydney? 
  
Join one of Australia’s leading universities at a pivotal point of change, where technology is evolving to better support student learning, empower teaching excellence, and enable research delivery at global scale. 
  
ICT is central to delivering the University’s academic mission. Working across faculties, disciplines, and research communities, you will shape the platforms, services, and partnerships that enable student success, strengthen teaching, and accelerate research impact. 
  
This work spans both the critical services that keep the University running every day and the ongoing improvement of systems and experiences. 
  
With strong investment and clear direction, you’ll operate in a large and complex environment where your contribution has visible, meaningful impact across the University. 
  
You’ll collaborate across academic and professional communities to help shape a more connected and capable University, supporting teaching excellence, research impact, and student success. 
  
The opportunity 
  
As Manager, Research Systems, you will lead the strategic management and ongoing improvement of the University’s research administration systems, supporting areas such as grants, ethics, academic profiles, research outputs and related operational processes. This is a newly created leadership role with the opportunity to shape both the function and the team as the University modernises a complex legacy landscape. 
  
You will play a key role in operationalising myResearch Sydney, a flagship Digital Sydney initiative. More than a replacement for IRMA, myResearch Sydney is a new integrated research administration system designed to simplify the research lifecycle by bringing together the people, processes and technology that support research administration, with staged delivery informed by close consultation with researchers and professional staff. 
  
What you’ll do 

Lead the roadmap, support, enhancement, and lifecycle management of the University’s research systems  
Manage a specialist team responsible for application support, maintenance, and continuous improvement  
Partner with research stakeholders and ICT teams to improve integration, service quality, and user experience  
Support the delivery and operationalisation of My Research Sydney and related initiatives  
Oversee governance, reporting, prioritisation, and issue resolution across the research systems portfolio  
What we’re looking for 

Experience leading platforms, systems, or application services in a complex ICT environment
Strong people leadership skills, with the ability to build capability and lead through change 
Background in service management, operational support, and continuous improvement 
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including the ability to work across technical and business teams 
Experience in a research-intensive, higher education, or similarly complex environment is beneficial  
What’s in it for you? 
  
At the University of Sydney, we support our staff with a range of benefits designed to enhance your career, wellbeing and work-life balance, including: 

Up to 17% superannuation to support your financial future  
Extremely generous parental leave entitlements  
Competitive salary with salary packaging options  
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements  
Career development and learning opportunities  
Health and wellbeing programs including Fitness Passport  
Access to a vibrant, globally recognised university community  
How to apply 
  
If you’re looking for an opportunity to contribute to meaningful projects while helping shape the future of research technology in a world-class university environment, we would love to hear from you. 
  
Accessibility and inclusion 

The University of Sydney is committed to creating an inclusive recruitment experience for all candidates. If you require adjustments or additional support at any stage of the recruitment process, we encourage you to let us know. 
  
Pre-employment checks 

Your employment is conditional upon the completion of all role required pre-employment or background checks in terms satisfactory to the University. Similarly, your ongoing employment is conditional upon the satisfactory maintenance of all relevant clearances and background check requirements. If you do not meet these conditions, the University may take any necessary step, including the termination of your employment. 
  
Also, to meet the University’s obligations under the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Eliminate Gender-Based Violence you will be asked to declare if you have been investigated for, or found to engaged in, sexual harm or gender-based violence in the course of previous employment or in a legal process. 
  
Sponsorship / work rights for Australia 
  
You must have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this employment to be eligible to apply. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Manager within the risk function supporting the AUSPAC Modernisation Program, you will play a critical role in enabling transformation across QBE AUSPAC. This role is responsible for overseeing both delivery and delivered risk, ensuring effective risk management throughout implementation and post‑delivery.




About QBE 

At QBE, we get to the heart of what matters for our customers. And we do it all with a human touch.

We’re an international insurer with more than 13,000 people working across 26 countries – which means we’re big enough for your ambitions, yet small enough for you to make a real impact. It’s an exciting time. We’re building momentum towards our vision to become the most consistent and innovative risk partner.

What if you could have a positive impact – at work and in the world? As part of the QBE team, you’ll get to spend every day working with people who are passionate, talented and kind.




Your New Role

Develop and embed project risk management strategies across delivery and delivered risk, ensuring mitigation plans are practical, time‑bound, and actionable.

Proactively monitor project risks and the effectiveness of mitigation plans and controls, maintaining accurate risk registers and providing regular, high‑quality reporting.

Lead comprehensive project risk assessments in partnership with risk owners and project leaders across all project phases.

Clearly articulate risk positions, assessments, and mitigation strategies to stakeholders across the program and QBE AUSPAC business units.

Oversee risk trends and emerging issues, transforming insights into concise, actionable reporting for senior management and governance forums.

Collaborate closely with project and business teams to promote strong risk awareness and provide guidance on effective risk management practices and tools.




About You

A strong transformation mindset, with the ability to operate effectively in complex, high‑change environments.

Proven experience managing transformation risk across large‑scale, business‑led change programs, covering both delivery and delivered risk.

Demonstrated experience within the insurance or broader financial services industry, with a sound understanding of operating in regulated environments.

Together building prosperity in the Kimberley and beyond — Safety, Sustainability, Community: it’s in our DNA.
 

At KMS, success is not just what we do — it’s how we do it. We’re seeking a hands on, outcomes focused Principal Continuous Improvement to embed a culture of improvement and lift performance across the business. You’ll be a trusted advisor to senior leaders and own enterprise level programs that strengthen governance, sustainability and stakeholder confidence.

About the Project

Kimberley Mineral Sands Pty Ltd (KMS) is a 50/50 joint venture between Sheffield Resources Limited and YGH Australia Investment Pty Ltd (Yansteel). The Thunderbird Mineral Sands Operation (Thunderbird) is located mid-way between Derby and Broome, in the Kimberley region of Western Australia. 

Thunderbird is one of the largest and highest-grade zircon mineral sands deposits in the world and contains valuable heavy minerals including ilmenite, zircon, leucoxene, and rutile, which are extracted and exported via the Port of Broome. 

Our head office is based in Broome, and together we are focused on delivering economic and social benefits for the West Kimberley and its communities. We do this through living our values of Integrity, Accountability, Humility, Co-Creation and Inclusivity. 

About the Role

Reporting to the Head of Operations, this is an exciting opportunity for a Principal Continuous Improvement to join our growing mining company.

This role can be offered on a residential Bus In Bus Out (BIBO) or Fly In Fly Out (FIFO) arrangement. Relocation support and additional benefits will be provided should the successful candidate be interested in moving to the West Kimberley.

What you’ll lead

Enterprise Continuous Improvement (CI) framework: design, implement and monitor a practical improvement framework aligned to organisational strategy; track outcomes and benefits with clear reporting.
Stewardship (organisational “wiring”) Program: own the design, governance and activities cadence (balanced scorecards, dashboards, KPIs) to drive accountability, transparency and responsible resource management.
Priority improvement delivery: identify and prioritise opportunities; facilitate CI activities such as value stream mapping, problem solving and idea generation; lead compliant process enhancements and sustainment plans.
Culture & capability: coach and mentor leaders and teams in CI methods (Lean, Six Sigma); develop tools, templates and training that build capability.
HS&E and risk alignment: ensure initiatives align with policies, risk frameworks, compliance requirements and our zero harm expectations.

About You

Degree in Business, Management or related discipline.
8+ years’ experience in continuous improvement, process optimisation or organisational development in complex environments.
Certification in Lean/Six Sigma or equivalent experience.
Strong analytics with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.
Highly effective stakeholder engagement and communication; you build trust quickly and collaborate to deliver meaningful change.
Leadership qualities that fit our environment: resilience and adaptability, a practical problem solving mindset, persistence and strong emotional intelligence.

Why join KMS?

Impact at enterprise scale: shape systems that improve performance and stewardship across the business.
Exposure & trust: partner closely with senior leaders; your advice and delivery will be visible and valued.
Community & purpose: contribute to prosperity in the Kimberley and beyond, grounded in safety and sustainability

Our Commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

KMS is committed to advancing an inclusive and diverse workforce culture and environment where people feel comfortable to be themselves. We acknowledge the different skills, experiences, and perspectives that people from diverse backgrounds including, race, ethnicity, disability, age, gender identity and sexual orientation bring to the workplace, will only strengthen our workforce.

We strongly encourage applications from our key priority communities – Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, women, and people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

Collaborate closely with Sales, Professional Services, Customer Success, Product, Marketing and Leadership to maximise impact across customer engagements

Champion Docusign as a trusted, secure, and effective partner for government agreement processes

Ad Text:

This is your chance to step into a high-profile enterprise transformation program, helping deliver a major shift to a modern, cloud-based SAP platform. If you enjoy working in complex environments and know how to drive change that actually sticks this role is worth a look.

The Role

Ad Text:
The role



The Business Analyst partners with business and technology stakeholders to understand business goals, analyse problems and opportunities, and translate needs into clear requirements, early designs, and prototypes. The role supports the delivery of user-centred digital solutions by shaping product and process improvements, validating concepts early, and enabling efficient delivery through well-defined user stories, acceptance criteria, and solution options.



The team



APRA's Technology team delivers, maintains, and enhances essential information technology services that support the organisation's productivity, strategic objectives, and business unit priorities.

Technology partner with stakeholders to design, implement and maintain IT solutions that meet the needs of the organisation and ensuring these are secure and aligned with APRA's technology architecture.

Key accountabilities


Build strong relationships with business stakeholders to quickly understand objectives, pain points and desired outcomes, and identify where digital products and solutions can add value.

Translate business needs into functional and non-functional requirements, ensuring they are testable, feasible and aligned to outcomes.

Produce high-quality analysis artefacts such as user stories, acceptance criteria, process maps, and impact assessments to support delivery.

Facilitate solution option analysis including costs, benefits, risks, dependencies, and delivery impacts to inform decision-making.

Collaborate with business and technical teams to create early design concepts (e.g., wireframes, workflow diagrams, low-fidelity screens).

Develop and iterate prototypes to validate requirements, user journeys, and usability with stakeholders and end users early in the lifecycle.

Coordinate and support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) planning and execution, including test scenarios, defect triage, and acceptance sign-off.

Develop and maintain system, training, and user support resources to drive adoption and meet user needs and expectations.

Communicate changes clearly and tailor messages and materials to different audiences, including non-technical stakeholders.




About you


Demonstrated experience as a Technical Business Analyst working in digital delivery environments, with strong capability across discovery, requirements elicitation and high‑quality documentation.

Strong experience delivering digital solutions across platforms such as Microsoft 365, Power Platform, ServiceNow and Oracle Fusion, with hands‑on technical understanding to shape solution design, integrations and delivery outcomes.

Experience facilitating user research, journey mapping and applying human‑centred design approaches.

Proven experience delivering initiatives across multiple digital domains, including web platforms, workflow, automation and enterprise business products.

Solid understanding of end-to-end product lifecycle, digital architecture, agile methodologies and IT service delivery best practices.

Excellent facilitation, communication, negotiation and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence outcomes and gain agreement across diverse business and technical stakeholders.

Demonstrated ability to manage priorities, driving outcomes in an agile, business-focused, collaborative environment.




To work with us you need to be an Australian Citizen and have the ability to gain a Defence Clearance.



About APRA

Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) was established in 1998 as an independent statutory authority that supervises almost 1,200 financial institutions that manage $8.6 trillion in assets for Australians across the banking, insurance and superannuation sectors.

In overseeing the safety, competitiveness and stability of the financial system, we seek to recruit, develop and retain highly skilled professionals, who want to help shape financial services and protect the financial wellbeing of the Australian community. Our employee base of almost 900 come predominantly from the commercial financial services industry or other government agencies; as such, we have the feel of a small corporate organisation that can work flexibly and with agility.

At APRA, we're committed to providing an inclusive workplace where everyone belongs, feels valued and respected. We aspire to attract and foster diversity of background, thought, and experience, recognising that a broad range of perspectives, approaches and ideas makes us stronger, and better enables us to meet our obligation to protect the financial wellbeing of the Australian community. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, please inform at application stage so we can do our best to accommodate your requirements.

Experience with document management systems such as eDOCS.

Detailed knowledge of current and emerging Information Management trends, standards and practices including content service platforms, use of Microsoft platform and collaboration tools and managing their impact on IM.

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

What You’ll Be Doing

 

We’re looking for a curious and proactive Senior Advisor - First Line Risk to help us strengthen our enterprise-wide risk capability. You’ll sit at the heart of our Legal, Risk and Compliance team, partnering with leaders across two iconic businesses - Compare the Market and iSelect - to deliver smart, practical risk initiatives that genuinely lift how we operate.

 

With your focus squarely set on first line risk, you will: 

 

       Strengthen and maintain the Risk Management Framework
       Provide hands-on risk advisory support, turning complex inputs into clear, decision-ready outcomes
       Partner with business leaders to conduct risk self-assessments
       Help develop, evolve and maintain risk frameworks, standards and policies
       Oversee control testing and remediation to ensure risks are managed effectively
       Turn risk data and trends into clear insights and smart, targeted actions
        Lead engaging risk workshops that build understanding and support a positive risk culture

You’ll be joining a small, growing team in a developing risk environment, where there’s plenty of opportunity to shape how things are done and lots to build and improve.  This role is best suited to someone self-driven who enjoys rolling up their sleeves, is comfortable with ambiguity and thrives in an agile, evolving environment with lots of meaningful work to tackle.

 

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

 

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Free coffee on Mondays
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program

 

What Sets You Apart

 

You’re someone who brings energy, curiosity and a practical approach to risk - without overcomplicating things.  You’ll also have:

 

3 - 5 years’ experience in a risk-related role (regulator, fin services or similar)
Good understanding of APRA prudential standards
Comfortable working with risk frameworks and knowing how to make them work in real life
Confident building relationships with senior stakeholders, collaborating and influencing outcomes
Experience building risk policies from the ground up and supporting policy development through early stages
A continuous-improvement mindset - you like making things clearer, simpler and better
Strong communicator who can explain things clearly (verbal and written) and bring people on the journey

Wolters Kluwer’s Tax & Accounting division in APAC delivers authoritative political, tax and legal content to professional services firms and corporations across the region. The way these firms consume, integrate, and apply that content is changing rapidly. AI-powered workflows, agent-based systems, and new integration patterns like MCP (Model Context Protocol) are reshaping how professional content gets used in practice.

We’re hiring a Senior Product Manager to own the development of the next iteration of our content offerings. Champion innovation, customer-centricity, and operational excellence to deliver market-leading expert solutions for legal, accounting, and political information professionals in a market where technology and competition are ever-changing. You will drive customer and business value by leading the strategy, development, and evolution of CCH iKnowConnect and CCH Parliament, ensuring alignment to wider commercial strategies to drive revenue and renewals.

Key Responsibilities
Product Innovation

Work closely with the Associate Director, Content Solutions, to understand Wolters Kluwer’s strategic and commercial objectives and the evolving needs of tax, legal and political information markets.

Define, communicate and lead a compelling and innovative product vision and strategy that aligns with those objectives and needs, and delivers on our financial goals.

Champion the integration of emerging technologies to enhance product value and competitive differentiation, driving product adoption, usage and revenue growth.

Stay current on AI delivery patterns: LLM integration, retrieval-augmented generation, MCP, agentic workflows. Use that knowledge to drive innovation that doesn’t merely keep up but leapfrogs the competition. 
 

Customer & Market Insight

Deeply understand customer segments, pain points and workflow through direct customer engagement, research and data analysis in collaboration with the Go-To-Market (GTM) team. Run client discovery to understand use cases and business scenarios to inspire innovative product development, ensuring initiatives are grounded in validated customer problems, not assumptions or technology‑led solutions.

Use qualitative and quantitative insights to validate desirability, viability, and feasibility of new product ideas and enhancements before they enter the roadmap.

Ensure all new product ideas and enhancements drive commercial objectives. Sequence and prioritize client requests: what’s achievable now with existing infrastructure, what requires new investment, what needs a phased approach.

Understand market trends and competitor insights provided by the GTM team and how they impact current and future product development. Use those insights to ideate and innovate solutions to bring to market to drive revenue goals.
 

Product Development & Delivery

In consultation with the Associate Director, Content Solutions and the Commercial Manager (R&L), own the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and MVP definition through to launch, ongoing optimisation, and eventual sunset, working closely with Product Owners.

Develop and maintain outcome-based product roadmaps to prioritise initiatives that drive measurable customer and commercial impact.

Ensure robust release strategies, including beta programmes, phased rollouts, and feedback loops.

Manage both an internal team of product specialists and a remote team of developers.

Develop and maintain relationships with WK’s global technology and development teams and the North American product teams to identify technology and product developments that may be adapted for the APAC market.

Collaborate cross-functionally with design, engineering, content, sales and GTM teams to deliver high-quality releases, launches and customer communications.

Conduct internal and customer-facing webinars to demonstrate product features and functionality. 
 

Commercial & Customer Success

Support the Commercial Manager (R&L) in annual, quarterly and monthly outcome-based planning, preparing business cases to secure internal capital funding for strategic initiatives (in consultation with the Associate Director, Content Solutions). Manage the capex and opex budget to maximize returns.

Work with the Associate Director, Content Solutions, and Commercial Manager (R&L) to set, track and report on clear success metrics (customer adoption, retention, NPS, revenue, etc) for all initiatives with accountability for closing the loop between delivery, customer outcomes, and commercial performance.

Monitor product performance, proactively identifying opportunities for improvement and intervening as needed.

Partner with Customer Success, Sales, and Marketing to ensure go‑to‑market readiness, support effective product launches, and drive sustained adoption, engagement, and customer advocacy. 

Manage regular upstream reporting requirements with the Commercial Manager (R&L) incorporating customer insight and adoption learnings alongside financial performance.
 

Team Leadership, Collaboration & Stakeholder Management

Foster a high-performing, collaborative product team culture, ensuring alignment to vision, goals, and values.

Engage with stakeholders across the business, including executive sponsors, content leaders, technology, and commercial teams to ensure product development is aligned with strategic goals.

Work collaboratively with commercial leaders in the software portfolio to identify innovation opportunities and integrate content solutions into software products. 
 

What Success Looks Like: 

Wolters Kluwer APAC is acknowledged as a market leader in delivering innovative solutions.

Customers and stakeholders clearly understand the value proposition and strategic direction of CCH iKnowConnect and CCH Parliament.

Roadmaps are executed effectively and on time, with the team focused on initiatives that drive renewal and new sales.

Product success is defined, measured, and communicated, with continuous improvement based on data and feedback. 
 

About You:

Proven experience in product strategy and management, ideally in B2B SaaS or a professional services technology space. Tax, accounting, or legal domain experience is a plus but not required if you bring strong professional services or fintech context.

Experience with AI-driven product development and AI implementation. You can talk to a client’s technology leadership about LLMs, APIs, retrieval-augmented generation, and agent architectures without reading from a slide. Familiarity with MCP or a track record of picking up new technical integration patterns quickly.

You’ve operated in a global, matrixed organization and know how to get things done when your success depends on teams in other time zones and other reporting lines. Demonstrated ability to inspire and lead a team when delivering complex projects.

You’re comfortable when the playbook doesn’t exist yet - clients are asking for things they can’t fully describe. You help them figure out what they actually need and then find a path to deliver it.

You have a passion for innovation which is tempered by strong strategic thinking, analytical and problem-solving skills, and a commercial approach. You are flexible in your approach and take initiative rather than wait to be asked. 

Demonstrated ability to define and articulate a clear strategic vision in ambiguous environments, and to translate that vision into commercially viable, revenue‑generating deliverables that drive sustainable business growth.

Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to engage with customers and multiple stakeholders in a complex business. 

Our Values

Wolters Kluwer continuously strives for an inclusive company culture in which we attract, develop, and retain high-performing, productive, engaged, and diverse talent to deliver on our strategy. As a global company, having a diverse workforce from different backgrounds, nationalities, races, genders, gender identities, ages, sexual orientations, physical disabilities, religions, expertise, and talents is of the utmost importance.

We pride ourselves on our culture, which promotes inclusion, accessibility and flexible working arrangements.

Culture and Benefits

We care for our people and a part of that we offer:

Flexible Working Arrangement – Work from Anywhere and Hybrid Working (promoting work life balance)

Learning and Development opportunities

Access to health and wellness programs

Parental leave benefits that exceed legislative requirements

The opportunity to work within a global organization with experienced leaders

Ad Text:
About the Company 

SA Water is a successful, modern water utility wholly owned by the Government of South Australia. We employ more than 1,700 people across a broad range of disciplines who operate more than $14 billion worth of infrastructure to deliver essential water and sewerage services to more than 1.8 million South Australians.

We operate in a geographically and climatically diverse service area, with sites and locations from Ceduna through to Port Augusta, the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands, across to the Riverland, down to Mount Gambier, and most places in between.

Our strength and expertise have been shaped by our history. For more than 165 years, South Australians have depended on us to innovate and deliver efficient and sustainable water services. With nationally and internationally recognised industry expertise, we’re focused on understanding and meeting our customers’ expectations.

As well as safely delivering smart and reliable services for our customers of today, we innovate to shape the water solutions of the future that will support thriving economies and healthy communities.

We are driven by our values; we value our people, we do work that matters, we keep it real, we know that our work has impact and we are future focussed. Staying true to our values ensures that we consistently deliver positive outcomes for all South Australians.

About the job  

The Growth Intelligence - Analytics and AI Lead plays a pivotal role in shaping how data insights and AI are used to drive smarter decision-making across Growth. Leading a small team of data experts and contractors, this role will work as a Data Product Owner and is responsible for delivering high-quality analytics, building strong data foundations and enabling scalable, AI driven solutions.  

Acting as the key interface between our transformation partner and internal teams, you’ll ensure initiatives are grounded in our data environment and aligned to business priorities.  

This is a hands-on leadership role focused on simplifying complexity, automating processes, accelerating data product delivery and embedding efficient repeatable ways of working that create real impact.  

This is a max-term opportunity until June 2027. 

About the Person

You’re a pragmatic and curious leader who understands how data and analytics come together in practice. With a strong background in analytics, reporting, data engineering, you’re comfortable navigating complex environments and translating between technical teams and business stakeholders.  

You don’t need to be an AI expert, but you have genuine interest in its potential and how it can be applied to solve real problems. You led from the front, bringing others with you, and are driven by improving how things work, whether that through automation, improve processes or deliver continuous value through smarter use of insights.  

You’re equally comfortable setting direction and rolling up your sleeves to make it happen.  

About Growth

The Growth group is at the heart of shaping South Australia’s future. This team plays a key role in supporting the state’s housing and infrastructure growth by delivering trusted water services that unlock land for development, create thriving new communities, and sustain existing regions. With a strong focus on single-point accountability, they ensure efficiency, consistent decision-making, and seamless connections for our customers. By bringing expertise and innovation together, they help drive smarter solutions and stronger partnerships that support South Australia’s growth for generations to come.

In addition, your responsibilities will include: 

Fostering trust with our people, customers and community by providing expert advice and service that ensure we effectively communicate and engage audiences with our business strategy and its delivery across South Australia, every day. 

You will: 

Lead, mentor, coach, guide the team to deliver data driven insights faster. 
Ensure the intelligence analysts, contractors and other personnel within the team achieve business objectives and goals within agreed timeframes. 
Provide timely guidance, feedback, coaching and training to help team members strengthen specific knowledge/skill areas needed to accomplish a task or solve a problem.
Lead initiatives to improve the maturity of domain data modelling, Growth Intelligence and Transformation.  
Review and determine suitability of new, and emerging AI technology, solutions/algorithms/models and recommend to be adopted by the Growth Group. 
Collaborate with teams across Growth, Planning, Systems, Customer and Technology to support integrated planning and intelligence data product delivery. 

Your success in this role will come from: 

Tertiary qualification in Data, AI, Information Technology, Business, or a related discipline.
Demonstrated experience in supporting the implementation of data solutions that align with business objectives. 
Effective communication and people skills including an ability to collaborate to deliver improved business outcomes.
Intermediate skill level in Microsoft Azure, Collibra, Microsoft SQL, data integration, data modelling, and data architecture.  
Ability to lead the team manage multiple tasks and deadlines within a continuously improving organization.

Optimisation Specialist

As we continue to scale, we’re investing in how our technology supports the business to operate effectively into the future and deliver at pace. To support this, we’re looking for an Optimisation Specialist to join our Technology Services team.

This role sits within Technology Delivery and focusses on improving how systems, tools and workflows come together to support critical day-to-day operations across the Group.

It’s suited to someone who enjoys working closely with the business and helping optimise workflows so they are simpler, more effective, and well supported by our technology advancements, as we continue to expand into the McNab Group.

 

About the Role

Reporting to the Technology Delivery Manager, you’ll play a key role in supporting the uplift of technology and workflow initiatives across McNab, and the way we need to operate and communicate across functions as we scale.

You’ll work alongside business and technology teams to understand current workflows, identify friction points, and help shape practical improvements that align to broader technology priorities.

Key responsibilities include:

Partner with business and technology teams to understand how work flows across teams and identify opportunities to optimise workflows
Support the design and rollout of technology initiatives that improve efficiency, usability and consistency
Business analysis and translating business needs into clear, practical outputs that support system and workflow uplift
Contribute to the successful delivery of technology programs by ensuring solutions align to real-world ways of working
Work with stakeholders to embed improved workflows and tools into day-to-day operations
Facilitate working sessions to align on challenges, priorities and practical solutions
Support ongoing uplift of workflows and ways of working through feedback and insight
Help drive clarity and consistency in how work is carried out across teams, supported by technology

 

About You

You’re someone who enjoys sitting between business and technology, bringing clarity to how work gets done and focusing on what will work in practice.

You’re comfortable working across a range of stakeholders and can balance big-picture thinking with practical delivery.

You’ll bring:

Experience in business analysis, optimisation and automation preparation of workflows for technology initiatives or operational improvement within complex environments
Ability to understand how work flows across teams and translate this into clear, actionable improvements
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to build trust and influence outcomes across all levels
A pragmatic, solutions-focused mindset with a focus on usability and outcomes
Confidence working in environments where priorities evolve and collaboration is key

Experience in construction, property or similarly operational industries is beneficial, but not essential.

WorkCover Queensland is entering an exciting new chapter, with a renewed strategic focus and strong executive leadership driving our 2030 Strategy. As we deliver an enterprise-wide transformation, strong data management and governance will underpin our ability to make a lasting difference for Queenslander employers and employees.

Building WorkCover’s Data Future

As part of this transformation, WorkCover is establishing a new Data Office to uplift data maturity across the organisation. This collaborative, customer centric and outcomes focused function brings together specialist leads across governance and quality, working as one integrated team.

Moving away from a traditional centralised model, the Data Office operates through partnership and collaboration, embedding with business and delivery teams to enable trusted, high quality data that supports both business as usual excellence and future capability.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Manager, Data Office, the Lead, Data Office Quality is accountable for ensuring WorkCover’s data is trusted, fit for purpose and aligned to organisational goals.

As the organisation’s data quality authority, you set direction, provide expert guidance and influence how quality is embedded throughout the data lifecycle. You work across business units and transformation teams to uplift data maturity, enable confident decision making and support WorkCover’s move towards a more reliable, future ready data ecosystem.

This role exists to build confidence in data, reduce risk and ensure quality is not an afterthought, but an embedded capability across the organisation.

In this role you will:

Lead day to day data quality operations, ensuring reliable and trusted data across transformation initiatives.
Define, embed and maintain data quality standards, frameworks and controls.
Act as the central escalation point for data quality issues, risks and exceptions.
Monitor, profile and report on data quality, identifying trends, risks and systemic issues.
Lead root cause analysis and guide teams to implement sustainable remediation.
Partner with delivery teams to ensure data products meet quality standards prior to release.
Collaborate with business, analytics and transformation teams to drive consistent quality outcomes.
Drive continuous improvement and uplift data quality maturity across the organisation.

About You

You are an experienced data quality leader with a strong transformation mindset and a passion for building confidence in data. You thrive on collaboration, bring structure to complexity and influence outcomes through expertise and engagement. You bring:

Extensive experience leading data quality in complex or regulated environments.
Strong knowledge of data quality frameworks, governance, privacy and regulatory controls.
Proven ability to diagnose issues and deliver sustainable remediation.
Confidence embedding quality practices into BAU operations and transformation initiatives.
Excellent communication skills across technical and non technical audiences.
A collaborative, coaching leadership style with a continuous improvement mindset.

Why Join Us

Our vision is to be the best workers’ compensation insurer, making a positive difference to people’s lives and keeping Queenslanders working. At WorkCover, we deliver with heart, own our outcomes, work best together and stay open to what’s possible - and we’re looking for people who share these values

When you join us, you’ll benefit from:

A purpose driven organisation where your work genuinely makes a positive impact on Queenslanders’ lives
A collaborative and supportive team environment that values empathy, trust and working together
Hybrid work arrangements that support flexibility and work–life balance
Personalised learning and development opportunities to support your growth and career progression
A holistic health and wellbeing program designed to support you both professionally and personally
Industry competitive salaries
A generous superannuation scheme, including co contribution
Study assistance to support ongoing education and professional development

You can find out more about what it’s like to work with us by visiting Working at WorkCover Qld.  

How do I apply?

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you. Applications close Friday 8th May at 5pm. 

We are committed to ensuring WorkCover reflects the diversity of the Queensland community. We welcome applications from First Nations peoples, members of the LGBTQIA+SB community, people of all ages, people who are neurodivergent, people with disability, and people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

Acceptance of applications submitted after advertising closes is at the discretion of the selection panel.

To ensure you have the best and most inclusive experience, please let us know if you require any reasonable adjustments or accommodations to help you confidently showcase your skills and potential. You can contact us at recruitment@workcoverqld.com.au or let our recruitment team know during your conversation with them.

Pre-employment checks will be conducted on all prospective employees. This includes a Right to Work in Australia Check, Police Check, Education Check, Reference Check, and where appropriate a Visa Check. The information provided will be treated as confidential in accordance with the Information Privacy Act 2009 (Qld). All permanent appointments are subject to a probationary period in accordance with our policy.
